title: Bitter Tender Malaysian Ground Beef Stir-fry
description: A weeknight-friendly Malaysian dish featuring tender ground beef cooked in a bitter sauce for added flavor and texture, suitable for those on the paleo diet.
instructions: Heat a wok or frying pan over medium-high heat and add oil.
Add ground beef to the pan and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Stir in minced garlic and cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant but not burnt.
Pour in kecap manis (Indonesian sweet soy sauce) and sambal belacuan (Malaysian chili paste), stir well to combine, and let simmer for a minute or two until the sauce has slightly thickened.
Stir in finely chopped onion and cook for another 2-3 minutes until the onions are translucent but not browned.
Fold in finely chopped cilantro and serve hot as a main dish with steamed rice.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
